questions.text: Many elements have fractional atomic masses. This is because:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: Introduction to Fundamental Concepts of Chemistry

options.text: Mass of atom is itself reactional
options.explanation: The mass of an atom is not reactional. There is no such mass to be said as reactional. Hence, this option is incorrect.

options.text: Atomic masses are average masses of isobars
options.explanation: Isobars are atoms that have the same mass number but a different number of protons. The fractional atomic mass of an element is not due to the average masses of isobars. But due to the average masses of its isotopes.

options.text: Atomic masses are average masses of isotopes
options.explanation: Isotopes are the atoms having same proton but different neutron numbers. The fractional atomic mass of an element is not due to the average masses of isotopes. But due to the average masses of its isotopes.

options.text: Atomic masses are average masses of isotopes proportional to their abundance
options.explanation: Atomic masses are typically reported as weighted averages that take into account the abundance of each isotope of an element. Most elements exist as a mixture of isotopes, and these isotopes have different masses. The atomic mass of an element listed on the periodic table is a weighted average of the masses of its naturally occurring isotopes. The weighting is based on the abundance of each isotope in nature. Since isotopes have different masses, the atomic mass is not a whole number, and it may appear as a fractional value.

questions.explanation: Atomic masses are typically reported as weighted averages that take into account the abundance of each isotope of an element. Most elements exist as a mixture of isotopes, and these isotopes have different masses. The atomic mass of an element listed on the periodic table is a weighted average of the masses of its naturally occurring isotopes. The weighting is based on the abundance of each isotope in nature. Since isotopes have different masses, the atomic mass is not a whole number, and it may appear as a fractional value.

questions.text: In which of the following will water evaporate earlier?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: Introduction to Fundamental Concepts of Chemistry

options.explanation: The rate of water evaporation depends on various factors such as the surface area exposed to air, temperature, humidity, and airflow. In general, water will evaporate more quickly in conditions where these factors promote evaporation. If we assume that the initial conditions (temperature, humidity, etc.) are the same for all containers, then the primary factor influencing the rate of evaporation would be the surface area exposed to air. Larger surface areas allow for more water molecules to be in contact with the air, promoting faster evaporation. A wide saucepan typically has a larger surface area compared to a cup, glass, or small bowl. Therefore, under similar conditions, water is likely to evaporate earlier from a saucepan than from the other containers.

questions.text: Different kinds of atoms of the same element are called isotopes having different _ but same _ properties.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: Introduction to Fundamental Concepts of Chemistry

options.explanation: Different kinds of atoms of the same element, which have the same number of protons but different numbers of neutrons (resulting in different masses), are called isotopes. Isotopes of the same element have different physical properties, such as different atomic masses and, in some cases, different nuclear stabilities. However, isotopes of the same element have the same chemical properties. This is because chemical properties are primarily determined by the number and arrangement of electrons, which is determined by the number of protons in the nucleus. Since isotopes of the same element have the same number of protons, they exhibit the same chemical behavior.

questions.explanation: Different kinds of atoms of the same element, which have the same number of protons but different numbers of neutrons (resulting in different masses), are called isotopes. Isotopes of the same element have different physical properties, such as different atomic masses and, in some cases, different nuclear stabilities. However, isotopes of the same element have the same chemical properties. This is because chemical properties are primarily determined by the number and arrangement of electrons, which is determined by the number of protons in the nucleus. Since isotopes of the same element have the same number of protons, they exhibit the same chemical behavior.

questions.text: The unit used to express the relative atomic mass is called?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: Introduction to Fundamental Concepts of Chemistry

options.explanation: The gram (originally gramme; SI unit symbol g) is a unit of mass in the International System of Units (SI) equal to one one thousandth of a kilogram.

Avogadro's number, number of units in one mole of any substance (defined as its molecular weight in grams), equal to 6.02214076 × 1023. The units may be electrons, atoms, ions, or molecules, depending on the nature of the substance and the character of the reaction (if any).
The quantity of matter contained in an atom of an element is atomic mass.

One atomic mass unit (1u) is a mass unit equal to exactly one-twelfth (1/12th) the mass of one atom of carbon-12 isotope, which is the scale used to compare atomic masses. So, the unit used to express relative atomic mass is amu.

questions.explanation: One atomic mass unit (1u) is a mass unit equal to exactly one-twelfth (1/12th) the mass of one atom of carbon-12 isotope, which is the scale used to compare atomic masses. So, the unit used to express relative atomic mass is amu. Thus, option D is correct.

questions.text: Which of the following has six isotopes?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: Introduction to Fundamental Concepts of Chemistry

options.explanation: Manganese has 27 isotopes. Hence, this option is incorrect.
Tin has 10 isotopes. Hence, this option is incorrect.
Calcium has 6 isotopes. As we have to find an element that has 6 isotopes, this option is the correct option.
Carbon has 3 isotopes. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Calcium has six stable isotopes: 40, 42, 43, 44, 46, 48Manganese has four stable isotopes: 52, 53, 54, 55Tin has ten isotopes: 112, 114, 115, 116, 117, 118, 119, 120, 122, 124Carbon has three isotopes: 12, 13, 14.So option C is correct.

questions.text: How many molecules are there in 2.1 moles of CO2:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: Introduction to Fundamental Concepts of Chemistry

options.explanation: Since there are 2.1 moles of CO2, we can easily find out the number of molecules present in it by multiplying 2.1 with the Avagadro's constant, which is 6.02 × 10²³. Hence, the answer would be approximately 1.2 x 10²⁴. The answer choice that is closest to the obtained value is 1.26 x 10²⁴. Please do not calculate the total number of atoms in this question; you have only been asked to calculate the total number of molecules.

questions.explanation: Since there are 2.1 moles of CO₂, we can easily find out the number of molecules present in it by multiplying 2.1 with the Avagadro's constant, which is 6.02 × 10²³. Hence, the answer would be approximately 1.2 x 10²⁴. The answer choice that is closest to the obtained value is 1.26 x 10²⁴. Please do not calculate the total number of atoms in this question; you have only been asked to calculate the total number of molecules.

questions.text: Avogadro's constant is the number of:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: Introduction to Fundamental Concepts of Chemistry

options.explanation: 2g of H equals two moles. Each mole of H atoms has 1 mole of electrons only, so two moles of H have twice the number of electrons than Avogadro's number.

Avogadro's Number (NA) is the number of atoms, molecules, ions or anything in one mole of the respective substance. As 6.02 x 1023 atoms, molecules, ions or electrons are present in one mole; therefore, this number is known as Avogadro's number. Only 24g of Mg equals one mole of Mg. Thus, only 24g of Mg has Avogadro's number of atoms. 1g of He equals 1/2 mole of He. Thus it has half the number of atoms than Avogadro's number. 35.5g of chlorine equals 1/2 mole of chlorine gas. Thus, it has half the number of molecules than Avogadro's number. 2g of H equals two moles. Each mole of H atoms has 1 mole of electrons only, so two moles of H have twice the number of electrons than Avogadro's number.
1g of He equals 1/2 mole of He. Thus it has half the number of atoms than Avogadro's number.
35.5g of chlorine equals 1/2 mole of chlorine gas. Thus, it has half the number of molecules than Avogadro's number.

questions.explanation: Avogadro's Number (NA) is the number of atoms, molecules, ions or anything in one mole of the respective substance. As 6.02 x 1023 atoms, molecules, ions or electrons are present in one mole; therefore, this number is known as Avogadro's number. Only 24g of Mg equals one mole of Mg. Thus, only 24g of Mg has Avogadro's number of atoms. 1g of He equals 1/2 mole of He. Thus it has half the number of atoms than Avogadro's number. 35.5g of chlorine equals 1/2 mole of chlorine gas. Thus, it has half the number of molecules than Avogadro's number. 2g of H equals two moles. Each mole of H atoms has 1 mole of electrons only, so two moles of H have twice the number of electrons than Avogadro's number.

questions.text: Find the mass in grams of 1.69 mol of phosphoric acid:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: Introduction to Fundamental Concepts of Chemistry

options.explanation: Moles = Mass / molar mass Moles x molar mass = mass in grams Molar mass of phosphoric acid H₃PO₄ = 98 g / mol 1.69 mol x 98 g /mol = mass in grams Mass = 165.6 g

questions.explanation: Moles = Mass / molar mass Moles x molar mass = mass in grams Molar mass of phosphoric acid H₃PO₄ = 98 g / mol 1.69 mol x 98 g /mol = mass in grams Mass = 165.6 g

questions.text: Natural gas in homes burns in excess of oxygen, what is the limiting reactant in this case?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: Introduction to Fundamental Concepts of Chemistry

options.explanation: Natural gas is obviously the limiting reactant when the gas is burning in excess of oxygen.

questions.explanation: Natural gas is obviously the limiting reactant when the gas is burning in excess of oxygen.

questions.text: Spectrometry is used when reactants and products absorb _.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: Introduction to Fundamental Concepts of Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is correct. Spectrometry is used when reactants and products absorb ultraviolet (UV) radiations. Ultraviolet-visible (UV-Vis) spectrometry is a common technique used to analyze the absorption of UV or visible light by chemical substances.

This option is also correct. Spectrometry can be used when reactants and products absorb visible light. UV-Vis spectrometry covers both ultraviolet and visible regions of the electromagnetic spectrum.

This option is not correct. Infrared (IR) spectrometry is used to study the vibrations of chemical bonds in molecules and is primarily used for identifying functional groups in organic compounds, rather than measuring absorbance of reactants and products.

This option is correct. Spectrometry can be used for analyzing the absorption of UV, visible, and infrared radiations by different chemical substances, depending on the specific technique and the properties of the molecules being studied.

questions.explanation: Spectroscopy is a method of understanding molecules by measuring the interaction of light and matter. By analyzing the amount of light absorbed or emitted by a sample, we can determine the sample's components, characteristics and volume.It can absorb UV radiations, visible radiations and infrared radiations.

questions.text: Formula mass is considered for _ compounds instead of their molecular mass:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: Introduction to Fundamental Concepts of Chemistry

options.explanation: "Metallic" is incorrect because metallic compounds are composed of metal atoms bonded together by metallic bonds, not ionic or covalent bonds.
The correct answer is "Ionic" Formula mass is considered for compounds instead of molecular mass for ionic compounds.
"Covalent" is incorrect because covalent compounds are formed by the sharing of electrons between atoms, and the concept of formula mass is not typically used for covalent compounds.
"Polar covalent" is incorrect because polar covalent compounds also involve the sharing of electrons between atoms, and the concept of formula mass is not typically used for these compounds either.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is "Ionic" Formula mass is considered for compounds instead of molecular mass for ionic compounds.

questions.text: Molecular ions are produced by passing a high-energy electron beam or an x-ray beam through:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: Introduction to Fundamental Concepts of Chemistry

options.explanation: Atoms: When x-rays pass through atoms, they can cause the ionization of individual atoms, resulting in the formation of atomic ions. However, the question specifically mentions molecular ions, which are formed from molecules rather than individual atoms. So, this option is incorrect.
Ions: X-rays do not typically produce molecular ions when passing through pre-existing ions.

The correct answer is "gas," specifically a gaseous substance. When x-rays are passed through a gas, such as air or a gas sample, they can cause the ionization of atoms within the gas. This ionization process occurs when the x-rays transfer enough energy to the gas atoms, causing them to lose one or more electrons and form positively charged ions. The resulting ions are called molecular ions because they are formed from molecules in the gas.

Solid: X-rays passing through a solid material primarily interact with the atoms within the solid. The ionization of atoms can occur, leading to the formation of atomic ions, but the question specifically asks for molecular ions

questions.explanation: The ion that is produced when a molecule loses or gains one or more electrons is called a molecular ion, e.g., CH4+, CO+, or N2+. Cationic molecular ions are more abundant than anionic molecular ions. When a high-energy electron beam or an X-ray beam passes through a gas, it can produce molecular ions. The high-energy particles, either electrons or X-ray photons, interact with the gas molecules, resulting in ionization. In the case of electron ionization, a high-energy electron beam is directed at a gaseous sample. The electrons collide with the gas molecules, leading to the removal of one or more electrons from the molecules.

questions.text: A compound has an empirical (simple) formula, C2H2O. If the experimental molecular weight is found to be in the range 160–170, the molecular formula of this compound is: (Atomic weight: C = 12.1, H = 1.008, O = 16).
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: Introduction to Fundamental Concepts of Chemistry

options.explanation: suggests that the molecular formula weight is three times the empirical formula weight, which would be:3 × 42.04 = 126.12 g/molHowever, the experimental molecular weight range given is 160–170 g/mol, which does not match the calculated molecular formula weight. Therefore, option A is incorrect.

C4H4O2Following the same calculations as above, the molecular formula weight for C4H4O2 would be:4 × 42.04 = 168.16 g/molMatches the experimental molecular weight range of 160–170 g/mol, so it is plausiblemolecular formula for the compound. However, we need to consider other options as well beforeconfirming.

You can use a trick called the "Doubling and Subtraction" method:Determine the empirical formula for mass:The empirical formula C2H2O has a mass of 2(12.01) + 2(1.01) + 16.00 = 44.03 g/mol.Calculate the empirical formula's "repeating factor":Divide the experimental molecular weight range (160–170) by the empirical formula mass (44.03).The range divided by the empirical formula for mass gives a range of 3.63 to 3.86.Round the repeating factor to the nearest whole number:In this case, the repeating factor is approximately 4.Multiply the subscripts in the empirical formula by the repeating factor:Multiply C2H2O by 4 to get C8H8O4, which is the molecular formula.

C6H6O3Using the same calculations as before, the molecular formula weight for C6H6O3 would be:6 × 42.04 = 252.24 g/molDoes not match the experimental molecular weight range of 160–170 g/mol, so it is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The empirical mass formula is 42, i.e., [2(12) + 2(1) + 16]. Multiplying the empirical formula mass by 4 will result in a molecular mass of 168, which is between 160 and 170, and would give us the molecular mass of C8H8O4.

questions.text: If the number of molecules of different gases are the same at S.T.P., the occupied volume will be _:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: Introduction to Fundamental Concepts of Chemistry

options.explanation: Volume of 1 mole of any ideal gas at STP=22.414dm³ ●1 mol of H2=6.02×10²³ molecules=22.414dm³ volume ●1 mol of O2=6.02×10²³molecules=22.41dm³ volume
Equal moles of any gas at STP have same volume. •1 molecule of N2 and 1mol of H2 have same volume at STP.
Same moles of ideal gas at STP have same volume
Same moles of ideal gas at STP have same volume.

questions.explanation: At standard temperature and pressure, 1 mole of gas has a volume of 22.4 dm3. This means that every gas occupies the same volume provided that the number of moles is the same.

questions.text: Actual yield is always less than theoretical yield due to:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: Introduction to Fundamental Concepts of Chemistry

options.explanation: A is incorrect since operational losses alone are not responsible for the loss in actual yield.
B is incorrect since reaction being reversible alone is not responsible for the loss in actual yield.
C is incorrect since side reactions alone are not responsible for the loss in actual yield.
D is correct since all three of these reasons are responsible for the actual yield being less than the theoretical yield.

questions.explanation: Option D is correct since all three of these reasons are responsible for the actual yield being less than the theoretical yield. Option A is incorrect since operational losses alone are not responsible for the loss in actual yield.Option B is incorrect since reaction being reversible alone is not responsible for the loss in actual yield.Option C is incorrect since side reactions alone are not responsible for the loss in actual yield.

questions.text: The bond formed when two or more atoms complete their electron shells by sharing electrons is called _:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Introduction to Fundamental Concepts of Chemistry

options.explanation: Ionic bond: An ionic bond is a chemical bond that occurs when an atom transfers an electron to another atom. This results in the formation of ions, which have opposite charges and are attracted to each other.
Hydrogen bond: A hydrogen bond is a weak bond that occurs between a hydrogen atom and a highly electronegative atom, such as oxygen or nitrogen. It is not a bond in which electrons are shared.
Covalent bonding has a characteristic feature that involves the sharing of electrons between two non-metal atoms.

options.text: None of these options are correct
options.explanation: Option C is correct.

questions.explanation: Covalent bonding has a characteristic feature which involves the sharing of electrons between two non-metal atoms.

questions.text: In stoichiometry, we follow law of _ while doing calculations:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: Stoichiometry

options.explanation: In stoichiometry, the law of conservation of mass and law of definite proportions are obeyed.

questions.explanation: In stoichiometry , the law of conservation of mass and law of definite proportions are obeyed.
